description:
# Skeleton step — types + unit tests, no wiring

## What changes

New module (probably `src/pattern_matrix.rs`):

```rust
pub struct Row {
    pub patterns: Vec<Spanned<Pattern>>,
    pub guard: Option<Spanned<Expr>>,
    pub body_id: BodyId,  // opaque handle; matrix doesn't lower bodies
}

pub struct Matrix {
    pub subjects: Vec<Var>,
    pub rows: Vec<Row>,
}

pub enum Decision {
    Leaf(BodyId),
    Switch {
        subject: Var,
        kind: SwitchKind,  // TupleArity | Atom | Int | Bool | ListIsNil
        cases: Vec<(SwitchKey, Box<Decision>)>,
        default: Box<Decision>,
    },
    Bind {
        subject: Var,
        name: String,
        next: Box<Decision>,
    },
    Project {
        subject: Var,
        kind: ProjectKind,  // TupleField(u32) | ListHead | ListTail | MapGet(Var)
        result: Var,
        next: Box<Decision>,
    },
    Guard {
        body_id: BodyId,
        guard: Spanned<Expr>,
        on_pass: Box<Decision>,  // execute body
        on_fail: Box<Decision>,  // continue to next clause
    },
    Fail,
}

pub fn compile(matrix: Matrix) -> Decision { ... }
```

`compile()` implements Maranget-lite:
- empty rows → Fail
- first row all-wildcard (or all-bind) → Leaf with any binds threaded
- pick leftmost column with non-wildcard; classify discriminator;
  specialize matrix per constructor; recurse; default = recurse on
  wildcards-only rows
- bitstring/map columns → no specialize, drop to per-row fallback marker
  (a Switch with one case + default)

## What stays

Nothing here yet — this ticket is types and unit tests only.

## What's removed

Nothing here yet.

## Acceptance

- Unit test: 3-row matrix with tuple patterns of arities {2, 3, 3}
  compiles to a Switch on TupleArity with 2 cases (the arity-3 case is
  a nested Switch or Leaf chain on atom field-0).
- Unit test: all-wildcard rows compile to first-row Leaf.
- Unit test: bitstring row compiles to per-row fallback marker.
- Unit test: guarded row compiles to Guard node with on_pass/on_fail.
- No fixture goldens touched — no wiring yet.

## Why now

The data types alone don't break anything. Lets the next ticket (.43.D)
focus purely on lowering, not also on inventing the IR.

## Risk

Designing the wrong abstraction. Mitigation: validate against the three
call sites (lower_multi_clause, lower_case, lower_with) — the types
must serve all three. Specifically `Matrix::subjects` is plural so
lower_multi_clause's multi-param matches work natively.
title: fz-ul4.43.C: PatternMatrix data types
